                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Background\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'You are building a data-ingestion pipeline for a product catalog powered by https://typesense.org. Typesense exposes a bulk `import` endpoint that accepts newline-delimited JSON (JSONL). Real-world source data is messy: some records have field values that Typesense can automatically coerce, some are broken in ways that can be repaired with well-defined business rules, and some are simply unusable. Your job is to import a large dataset robustly, recover the documents that can be salvaged, and produce an accurate ingestion report.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'A key subtlety of the Typesense import endpoint is that it always returns `HTTP 200` even when individual documents fail. The response body is JSONL with **one result line per input document, in the same order** as the request. A successful line looks like `{"success": true}` and a failed line looks like `{"success": false, "error": "...", "document": "..."}`. A correct pipeline must therefore inspect the per-document results rather than relying on the HTTP status code.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Requirements\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Ensure a Typesense server is running and create a collection named `catalog` with a strict, explicitly-typed schema (all fields required):\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `sku` (`string`)\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `name` (`string`)\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `price` (`float`)\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `quantity` (`int32`)\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `category` (`string`)\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * The document identifier is the special `id` field.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Bulk-import the provided JSONL dataset using the import API. Enable dirty-value handling that **coerces** compatible type mismatches (e.g. a numeric value provided as a JSON string) but **rejects** (never silently drops) values it cannot coerce, so that genuinely invalid documents surface as per-document failures.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Parse the per-document import response, detect every failed document, and collect the corresponding source records.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Apply repair transformations to the fixable failures and re-import **only** the repaired documents. Documents whose failures are not addressed by the repair rules must stay failed and must **not** end up indexed in the collection.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Write an accurate success/failure report.\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'## Implementation Hints\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Use any language/SDK you like (a Python or Node.js SDK, or plain HTTP calls to the REST API are all fine). Interact with the real Typesense server — do not fake the import results.\n'[39m +
                [32m"* The import endpoint's response order matches the input order; map each result line back to its source document to identify failures. The failed line also echoes the offending document under the `document` key.\n"[39m +
                [32m'* Treat the two repair rules below as the *only* fixes your pipeline is allowed to apply. Any failed document not fully repaired by these rules must be reported as failed:\n'[39m +
                [32m'  1. **Currency-formatted price** — when `price` is a string that is not directly numeric because it contains a currency symbol and/or thousands separators (e.g. `"$1,299.00"`), normalize it by removing the currency symbol and thousands separators so it becomes a valid number.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  2. **Missing category** — when the required `category` field is absent from a document, set it to the literal string `"uncategorized"`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Make the pipeline idempotent: running it repeatedly must always yield the same correct report and the same final collection contents (for example, recreate the collection at the start and/or use upsert semantics).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* A Typesense standalone server binary is installed at `/usr/local/bin/typesense-server`. Ensure a server is reachable at `http://localhost:8108` with API key `xyz` (start it against a writable data directory if it is not already running).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Project path: `/home/user/import-pipeline`\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Command: `python3 pipeline.py` (or provide an equivalent entrypoint; the grader will invoke `python3 pipeline.py`).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Input dataset: `/home/user/import-pipeline/data/raw_products.jsonl` (JSONL, one document per line).\n'[39m +
                [32m'* Report file: `/home/user/import-pipeline/report.json`. Write a single JSON object with exactly these keys:\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `total` (integer): total number of documents in the input dataset.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `imported_first_pass` (integer): documents successfully indexed on the initial import (before any repair).\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `recovered` (integer): documents that failed the initial import but were successfully indexed after repair.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `failed` (integer): documents that were never successfully indexed.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `recovered_ids` (array of strings): the `id`s of the recovered documents, sorted in ascending order.\n'[39m +
                [32m'  * `failed_ids` (array of strings): the `id`s of the documents that remained failed, sorted in ascending order.\n'[39m +
                [32m'* The counts must be internally consistent: `imported_first_pass + recovered + failed == total`, and the final number of documents in the `catalog` collection must equal `imported_first_pass + recovered`.'[39m

                  output: [32m'root         201  0.0  0.0   4328  3524 ?        S    04:17   0:00 bash -c set -o pipefail; cat > /tmp/pochi_cmd.sh << \'CMD_EOF\' set -o pipefail pochi --model google/gemini-3.5-flash --max-steps 200 --max-retries 10 --blobs-dir /logs/agent/pochi/blobs --experimental-stream-trajectory /logs/agent/pochi/trajectory.jsonl --experimental-stream-trajectory-strip-duplicates > >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stdout.txt) 2> >(tee /logs/agent/pochi/stderr.txt >&2) <<\'EOF\' # Resilient JSONL Bulk-Import Pipeline with Selective Retry  ## Background You are building a data-ingestion pipeline for a product catalog powered by [Typesense](https://typesense.org). Typesense exposes a bulk `import` endpoint that accepts newline-delimited JSON (JSONL). Real-world source data is messy: some records have field values that Typesense can automatically coerce, some are broken in ways that can be repaired with well-defined business rules, and some are simply unusable. Your job is to import a large dataset robustly, recover the documents that can be salvaged, and produce an accurate ingestion report.  A key subtlety of the Typesense import endpoint is that it always returns `HTTP 200` even when individual documents fail. The response body is JSONL with **one result line per input document, in the same order** as the request. A successful line looks like `{"success": true}` and a failed line looks like `{"success": false, "error": "...", "document": "..."}`. A correct pipeline must therefore inspect the per-document results rather than relying on the HTTP status code.  ## Requirements - Ensure a Typesense server is running and create a collection named `catalog` with a strict, explicitly-typed schema (all fields required):   - `sku` (`string`)   - `name` (`string`)   - `price` (`float`)   - `quantity` (`int32`)   - `category` (`string`)   - The document identifier is the special `id` field. - Bulk-import the provided JSONL dataset using the import API. Enable dirty-value handling that **coerces** compatible type mismatches (e.g. a numeric value provided as a JSON string) but **rejects** (never silently drops) values it cannot coerce, so that genuinely invalid documents surface as per-document failures. - Parse the per-document import response, detect every failed document, and collect the corresponding source records. - Apply repair transformations to the fixable failures and re-import **only** the repaired documents. Documents whose failures are not addressed by the repair rules must stay failed and must **not** end up indexed in the collection. - Write an accurate success/failure report.  ## Implementation Hints - Use any language/SDK you like (a Python or Node.js SDK, or plain HTTP calls to the REST API are all fine). Interact with the real Typesense server ??? do not fake the import results. - The import endpoint\'s response order matches the input order; map each result line back to its source document to identify failures. The failed line also echoes the offending document under the `document` key. - Treat the two repair rules below as the *only* fixes your pipeline is allowed to apply. Any failed document not fully repaired by these rules must be reported as failed:   1. **Currency-formatted price** ??? when `price` is a string that is not directly numeric because it contains a currency symbol and/or thousands separators (e.g. `"$1,299.00"`), normalize it by removing the currency symbol and thousands separators so it becomes a valid number.   2. **Missing category** ??? when the required `category` field is absent from a document, set it to the literal string `"uncategorized"`. - Make the pipeline idempotent: running it repeatedly must always yield the same correct report and the same final collection contents (for example, recreate the collection at the start and/or use upsert semantics). - A Typesense standalone server binary is installed at `/usr/local/bin/typesense-server`. Ensure a server is reachable at `http://localhost:8108` with API key `xyz` (start it against a writable data directory if it is not already running). - Project path: `/home/user/import-pipeline` - Command: `python3 pipeline.py` (or provide an equivalent entrypoint; the grader will invoke `python3 pipeline.py`). - Input dataset: `/home/user/import-pipeline/data/raw_products.jsonl` (JSONL, one document per line). - Report file: `/home/user/import-pipeline/report.json`. Write a single JSON object with exactly these keys:   - `total` (integer): total number of documents in the input dataset.   - `imported_first_pass` (integer): documents successfully indexed on the initial import (before any repair).   - `recovered` (integer): documents that failed the initial import but were successfully indexed after repair.   - `failed` (integer): documents that were never successfully indexed.   - `recovered_ids` (array of strings): the `id`s of the recovered documents, sorted in ascending order.   - `failed_ids` (array of strings): the `id`s of the documents that remained failed, sorted in ascending order. - The counts must be internally consistent: `imported_first_pass + recovered + failed == total`, and the final number of documents in the `catalog` collection must equal `imported_first_pass + recovered`.   EOF CMD_EOF setsid --wait bash /tmp/pochi_cmd.sh & SETSID_PID=$! echo $SETSID_PID > /tmp/setsid_bash_pochi_cmd.pid wait $SETSID_PID 2>/dev/null\r\n'[39m +

                    text: [32m'# Collections\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2026-05-15, last updated: 2026-07-13, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/30.2/api/collections.html)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m"You want to NOT mention these fields in the collection's schema or mark these fields as `index: false` (see `fields` schema parameter below) to mark it as an unindexed field.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'#### # Indexing all but some fields\n'[39m +
                      [32m'If you have a case where you do want to index all fields in the document, except for a few fields, you can use the `{"index": false, "optional": true}` settings to exclude fields.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Note: it is not currently possible to have a mandatory field excluded from the indexing, hence the setting to optional.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'For eg, if you want to index all fields, except for fields that start with `description_`, you can use a schema like this:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'### # Field parameters\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|Parameter|Required|Description|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|--|--|--|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|name|yes|Name of the field.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|type|yes|The data type of the field (see the section below for a list of types).|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|facet|no|Enables faceting on the field.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Default: `false`.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|optional|no|When set to `true`, the field can have empty, null or missing values.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Default: `false`.|'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Optional field or default value ? · Issue #91 · typesense ...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2020-02-09, last updated: 2025-03-29, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense/issues/91)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'@geoffreyp v0.12.0 has landed and it has support for optional fields.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You can mark a field as "optional": true during declaration.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You can read more here: https://typesense.org/docs/0.12.0/api/#create-collection\n'[39m +
                      [32m'"optional": true\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Possible to allow null values in optional fields?\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m"Hi @skipjack, you'd need to remove the entire key from the document for optional fields, instead of setting it to null."[39m

                      [32m'}\n'[39m +

                      [32m'15 /// The name of an int32 / float field that determines the order in which\n'[39m +

                      [32m'30 pub name: String,\n'[39m +

                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# typesense_collection | Resources | Kekenika/typesense\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2021-11-05, last updated: 2026-06-16, [Read more](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/Kekenika/typesense/latest/docs/resources/collection)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'## Example Usage\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'resource "typesense_collection" "my_collection" {\n'[39m +
                      [32m'name = "my-collection"\n'[39m +

                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'## Schema\n'[39m +
                      [32m'### Required\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **fields** (Block List, Min: 1) (see below for nested schema)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **name** (String) Name of the collection.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'### Optional\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **default_sorting_field** (String)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **id** (String) The ID of this resource.\n'[39m +

                      [32m'- **num_documents** (Number)\n'[39m +

                      [32m'Required:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **name** (String)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **type** (String) Field type\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Optional:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **facet** (Boolean) Facetable field\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **index** (Boolean) Index field\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **optional** (Boolean) Optional field\n'[39m +
                      [32m'## Import'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Typesense API Reference for v29.1\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2026-04-21, last updated: 2026-07-03, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/29.1/)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m''[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Organizing Collections - Typesense\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2026-05-15, last updated: 2026-07-04, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/guide/organizing-collections.html)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'collection.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You can set non-common fields/attributes as optional in the collection schema.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Question - Is it possible to have optional query parameters? · Issue #1420 · typesense/typesense\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2023-12-08, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense/issues/1420)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Try marking the `dataExample` field as `optional` in the collection schema.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'For e.g.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{"name": "dataExample", "type": "string", "optional": true}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Query for documents where an optional field is not set ...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2022-11-14, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense/issues/790)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m"Typesense does not have a direct way to query for documents that don't have an optional field set.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'To solve for this, you want to create a new boolean field in the schema called say `is_fieldX_empty: true | false` and set it any time you create/update `fieldX`.\n'[39m +
                      [32m"You can then use `is_fieldX_empty` to do the query you're looking for.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'It might be faster to read the data from your primary datasource and then use the documents/import endpoint with `action= emplace` to just add a single field to your existing Typesense collection.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m"Doesn't fit my use-case but for anyone that wants to find all documents with unset optional numeric fields, you could use `filter_by` to arrange them at the top.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'You *can* also use `filter_by` to isolate documents with unset optional string fields.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Not recommended as it can require a lot of extra memory, particularly for long-string fields like description.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# typesense-collection-schema-generator/README.md at master · typesense/typesense-collection-schema-generator\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2024-02-05, last updated: 2025-05-20, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense-collection-schema-generator/blob/master/README.md)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Will generate an output schema like this:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{\n'[39m +
                      [32m'"name": "your_collection_name",\n'[39m +
                      [32m'"fields": [\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "organization_name", "type": "string", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "country", "type": "string", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "full_name", "type": "string", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "address_string", "type": "string", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "address", "type": "object", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "addresses", "type": "object[]", "optional": true },\n'[39m +
                      [32m'{ "name": "tags", "type": "string[]", "optional": true }\n'[39m +
                      [32m']\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Important\n'[39m +
                      [32m'This schema is not meant to be used as-is.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You want to review the generated schema, add\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'facet: true\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'for any facet fields, remove any un-indexed fields, or consider using auto-schema detection.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Consider using regex field names for repeated field definitions.'[39m
                  }
                ]

              toolCallId: [32m'UY97fBYYsb63JrsP'[39m,
              toolName: [32m'webSearch'[39m,
              output: {
                type: [32m'content'[39m,
                value: [
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Documents\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2023-02-27, last updated: 2026-06-24, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/0.21.0/api/documents.html)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'The `dirty_values` parameter determines what Typesense should do when the type of a particular field being\n'[39m +
                      [32m'indexed does not match the previously inferred type for that field.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|Value|Behavior|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|--|--|\n'[39m +
                      [32m"|`coerce_or_reject`|Attempt coercion of the field's value to previously inferred type.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'If coercion fails, reject the write outright with an error message.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m"|`coerce_or_drop`|Attempt coercion of the field's value to previously inferred type.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'If coercion fails, drop the particular field and index the rest of the document.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|`drop`|Drop the particular field and index rest of the document.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|`reject`|Reject the document outright.|\n'[39m +
                      [32m'**Default behaviour**\n'[39m +
                      [32m'If a wildcard (`.*`) field is defined in the schema *or* if the schema contains any field name with a regular expression (e.g a field named `.*_name`), the default behavior is `coerce_or_reject`.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Otherwise,\n'[39m +
                      [32m'the default behavior is `reject` (this ensures backward compatibility with older Typesense versions).\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Similarly, we can use the `dirty_values` parameter for the update and import operations as well.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Collections\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2026-05-15, last updated: 2026-07-13, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/30.2/api/collections.html)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m"This option gives you fine-grained control over your document fields' data types and configures your collection to reject documents that don't match the data types defined in your schema (by default).\n"[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'*By default,* Typesense will try to coerce (convert) the value to the previously inferred type.\n'[39m +
                      [32m"So in this example, since the first document had a numeric data-type for `popularity_score`, the second document's `popularity_score` field will be coerced to an integer from string.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'In such cases, when Typesense is unable to coerce the field value to the previously inferred type, the indexing will fail with the appropriate error.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'TIP\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You can control this default coercion behavior at write-time with the `dirty_values` parameter.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|symbols_to_index|no|List of symbols or special characters to be indexed.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'For e.g. you can add `+` to this list to make the word `c++` indexable verbatim.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'|default_sorting_field|no|The name of an `int32 / float` field that determines the order in which the search results are ranked when a `sort_by` clause is not provided during searching.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'This field must indicate some kind of popularity.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# [bug] documents.import_ function signature requires return_id or return_doc when neither is required · Issue #74 · typesense/typesense-python\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2025-03-29, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense-python/issues/74)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'action (str): [Action](https://typesense.org/docs/26.0/api/documents.html#action-modes-create-upsert-update-emplace) to perform on the document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- `create`: Creates a new document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Fails if a document with the same id\n'[39m +
                      [32m'already exists (default).\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- `upsert`: Creates a new document or updates an existing document if a\n'[39m +
                      [32m'document with the same id already exists.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Requires the whole document to be sent.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'You can send a partial document containing only the\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'dirty_values (str): [Handling of dirty values](https://typesense.org/docs/26.0/api/documents.html#dealing-with-dirty-data) in the document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m"- `coerce_or_reject`: Attempt coercion of the field's value to previously inferred type.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'If coercion fails, reject the write outright with an error message.\n'[39m +
                      [32m"- `coerce_or_drop`: Attempt coercion of the field's value to previously inferred type.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'If coercion fails, drop the particular field and index the rest of the document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- `drop`: Drop the particular field and index the rest of the document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- `reject`: Reject the write outright with an error message.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'"""'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Releases · typesense/typesense-go\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2025-03-27, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense-go/releases)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **[Breaking Change]** Single document index methods now include an additional parameter to specify dirty values behavior.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'client.Collection("companies").Documents().Create(context.Background(), document, &api.DocumentIndexParameters{DirtyValues: pointer.Any(api.CoerceOrDrop)})\n'[39m +
                      [32m'client.Collection("companies").Documents().Upsert(context.Background(), document, &api.DocumentIndexParameters{DirtyValues: pointer.Any(api.CoerceOrDrop)})\n'[39m +
                      [32m'client.Collection("companies").Document("123").Update(context.Background(), document, &api.DocumentIndexParameters{DirtyValues: pointer.Any(api.CoerceOrDrop)})\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **[Breaking Change]** An additional parameter `*api.ExportDocumentsParams` allows control over export results.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- **[Breaking Change]** The import `Action` type is now an enum instead of `*string`.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'&api.ImportDocumentsParams{\n'[39m +
                      [32m'// Action: pointer.String("create"),\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Action: pointer.Any(api.Create),\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'This is especially useful for parameters of enum types.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- The `q` and `query_by` params are now optional and use `pointer.String` type, which is a backward incompatible change.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Null values in nested fields cause schema update to fail · Issue #916 · typesense/typesense\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2023-02-28, last updated: 2026-03-21, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense/issues/916)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'With a nested array type such as a `string[]` on an `object`, if any *non-initial* values are `null` (created with `dirty_values:drop`, then subsequent attempts to `update()` the collection schema fail with: `RequestMalformed: [Errno 400]`.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Import data with non-initial null value:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'data = [{"id": "1", "lines": [{"name": "foo"}, {"name": None}]}]\n'[39m +
                      [32m'client.collections["test"].documents.import_(\n'[39m +
                      [32m'data, {"action": "upsert", "dirty_values": "drop"}\n'[39m +
                      [32m')\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Either the initial import should fail, or the unrelated schema change should pass.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Existing data for field `lines.name` cannot be coerced into an array of string'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# api package - github.com/guialbuquerque5/typesense ...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-24, [Read more](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/guialbuquerque5/typesense-go/typesense/api)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'const (\n'[39m +
                      [32m'CoerceOrDrop DirtyValues = "coerce_or_drop"\n'[39m +
                      [32m'CoerceOrReject DirtyValues = "coerce_or_reject"\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Drop DirtyValues = "drop"\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Reject DirtyValues = "reject"\n'[39m +
                      [32m')\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Defines values for DirtyValues.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'#### type ImportDocumentsParams ¶\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'type ImportDocumentsParams struct {\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Action *IndexAction `form:"action,omitempty" json:"action,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'BatchSize *int `form:"batch_size,omitempty" json:"batch_size,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'DirtyValues *DirtyValues `form:"dirty_values,omitempty" json:"dirty_values,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'RemoteEmbeddingBatchSize *int `form:"remote_embedding_batch_size,omitempty" json:"remote_embedding_batch_size,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'ReturnDoc *bool `form:"return_doc,omitempty" json:"return_doc,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'ReturnId *bool `form:"return_id,omitempty" json:"return_id,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'```\n'[39m +
                      [32m'type IndexDocumentParams struct {\n'[39m +
                      [32m'// Action Additional action to perform\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Action *IndexAction `form:"action,omitempty" json:"action,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'// DirtyValues Dealing with Dirty Data\n'[39m +
                      [32m'DirtyValues *DirtyValues `form:"dirty_values,omitempty" json:"dirty_values,omitempty"`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'}'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Syncing Data into Typesense\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2026-07-01, last updated: 2026-07-12, [Read more](https://typesense.org/docs/guide/syncing-data-into-typesense.html)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'3. On a periodic basis (say every 30s), query your database for all records that have an `updated_at` timestamp between the current time and the last time the sync process ran (you want to maintain this `last_synced_at` timestamp in a persistent store).\n'[39m +
                      [32m'4. Make a Bulk Import API call to Typesense with just the records in Step 3, with `action=upsert`\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'3. Set up a scheduled job(s) that run frequently (even as little as every 5-10 seconds):\n'[39m +
                      [32m'This job queries unprocessed records from the buffer table, groups them by operation type, sends inserts/updates as a bulk import API call with `action=upsert`, marks processed records in the buffer table, and optionally removes old processed records after a retention period.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m"In the import API call, you'll notice a parameter called `batch_size`.\n"[39m +
                      [32m'This controls server-side batching (how many documents from the import API call are processed, before the search queue is serviced), and you almost never want to change this value from the default.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Instead, you want to do client-side batching, by controlling the number of documents in a single import API call and potentially do multiple API calls in parallel.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# Hex Preview\n'[39m +

                      [32m'...\n'[39m +
                      [32m'schema:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'type: string\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- name: action\n'[39m +
                      [32m'in: query\n'[39m +
                      [32m'description: Additional action to perform\n'[39m +
                      [32m'schema:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'type: string\n'[39m +
                      [32m'example: upsert\n'[39m +

                      [32m'properties:\n'[39m +

                      [32m'dirty_values:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'type: string\n'[39m +
                      [32m'enum:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- coerce_or_reject\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- coerce_or_drop\n'[39m +
                      [32m'- drop\n'[39m +

                      [32m'required: true\n'[39m +
                      [32m'responses:\n'[39m +
                      [32m'200:\n'[39m +

                      [32m'Result of the import operation.'[39m
                  },
                  {
                    type: [32m'text'[39m,
                    text: [32m'# typesense-go/typesense/api/types_gen.go at master · typesense/typesense-go\n'[39m +
                      [32m'created: 2020-12-19, last updated: 2025-07-30, [Read more](https://github.com/typesense/typesense-go/blob/master/typesense/api/types_gen.go)\n'[39m +
                      [32m'\n'[39m +
                      [32m'// Defines values for DirtyValues.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'const (\n'[39m +

                      [32m')\n'[39m +

                      [32m'type DirtyValues string\n'[39m +
                      [32m'// DropTokensMode Dictates the direction in which the words in the query must be dropped when the original words in the query do not appear in any document.\n'[39m +
                      [32m'Values: right_to_left (default), left_to_right, both_sides:3 A note on both_sides:3 - for queries upto 3 tokens (words) in length, this mode will drop tokens from both sides and exhaustively rank all matching results.\n'[39m +

                  [32m'client = typesense.Client({\n'[39m +
                  [32m"  'nodes': [{\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    'host': 'localhost',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    'port': '8108',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    'protocol': 'http'\n"[39m +
                  [32m'  }],\n'[39m +
                  [32m"  'api_key': 'xyz',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"  'connection_timeout_seconds': 2\n"[39m +
                  [32m'})\n'[39m +
                  [32m'try:\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    client.collections['catalog'].delete()\n"[39m +
                  [32m'except Exception:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    pass\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'schema = {\n'[39m +
                  [32m"  'name': 'catalog',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"  'fields': [\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    {'name': 'sku', 'type': 'string'},\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    {'name': 'name', 'type': 'string'},\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    {'name': 'price', 'type': 'float'},\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    {'name': 'quantity', 'type': 'int32'},\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    {'name': 'category', 'type': 'string'}\n"[39m +
                  [32m'  ]\n'[39m +
                  [32m'}\n'[39m +
                  [32m'client.collections.create(schema)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'# Read raw products\n'[39m +
                  [32m"with open('/home/user/import-pipeline/data/raw_products.jsonl', 'r') as f:\n"[39m +
                  [32m'    raw_docs = [json.loads(line) for line in f]\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'total_docs = len(raw_docs)\n'[39m +
                  [32m"print('Total input docs:', total_docs)\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'# First pass import\n'[39m +
                  [32m"url = 'http://localhost:8108/collections/catalog/documents/import'\n"[39m +
                  [32m'params = {\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    'action': 'create',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    'dirty_values': 'coerce_or_reject'\n"[39m +
                  [32m'}\n'[39m +
                  [32m'headers = {\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    'X-TYPESENSE-API-KEY': 'xyz',\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    'Content-Type': 'application/octet-stream'\n"[39m +
                  [32m'}\n'[39m +
                  [32m"raw_jsonl = '\\n'.join(json.dumps(doc) for doc in raw_docs)\n"[39m +
                  [32m"response = requests.post(url, params=params, headers=headers, data=raw_jsonl.encode('utf-8'))\n"[39m +
                  [32m"lines = response.text.strip().split('\\n')\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'imported_first_pass = []\n'[39m +
                  [32m'failed_first_pass = [] # list of (index, doc, error_msg)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    res = json.loads(line)\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    if res.get('success'):\n"[39m +
                  [32m'        imported_first_pass.append(raw_docs[i])\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    else:\n'[39m +
                  [32m"        failed_first_pass.append((i, raw_docs[i], res.get('error', '')))\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m"print('Imported first pass:', len(imported_first_pass))\n"[39m +
                  [32m"print('Failed first pass:', len(failed_first_pass))\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'# Repair rules\n'[39m +
                  [32m'repaired_docs = []\n'[39m +
                  [32m'not_repaired_docs = []\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'for idx, doc, err in failed_first_pass:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    repaired_doc = dict(doc)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    repaired = False\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    \n'[39m +
                  [32m'    # Rule 1: Currency-formatted price\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    price = repaired_doc.get('price')\n"[39m +
                  [32m'    if isinstance(price, str):\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        # check if contains currency symbol or thousands separator\n'[39m +
                  [32m"        if any(c in price for c in ['$', '€', '£', '¥', ',']):\n"[39m +
                  [32m"            cleaned = price.replace('$', '').replace(',', '')\n"[39m +
                  [32m'            try:\n'[39m +
                  [32m"                repaired_doc['price'] = float(cleaned)\n"[39m +
                  [32m'                repaired = True\n'[39m +
                  [32m`                print(f'Repaired price for {repaired_doc[\\"id\\"]}: {price} -> {repaired_doc[\\"price\\"]}')\n`[39m +
                  [32m'            except ValueError:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'                pass\n'[39m +
                  [32m'                \n'[39m +
                  [32m'    # Rule 2: Missing category\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    if 'category' not in repaired_doc:\n"[39m +
                  [32m"        repaired_doc['category'] = 'uncategorized'\n"[39m +
                  [32m'        repaired = True\n'[39m +
                  [32m`        print(f'Repaired category for {repaired_doc[\\"id\\"]}')\n`[39m +
                  [32m'        \n'[39m +
                  [32m'    if repaired:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        repaired_docs.append((idx, repaired_doc))\n'[39m +
                  [32m'    else:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        not_repaired_docs.append((idx, doc))\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m"print('Repaired docs count:', len(repaired_docs))\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'# Re-import only repaired documents\n'[39m +
                  [32m'recovered_docs = []\n'[39m +
                  [32m"failed_docs = list(not_repaired_docs) # start with ones we couldn't even attempt to repair\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m'if repaired_docs:\n'[39m +
                  [32m"    repaired_jsonl = '\\n'.join(json.dumps(doc) for idx, doc in repaired_docs)\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    res_second = requests.post(url, params=params, headers=headers, data=repaired_jsonl.encode('utf-8'))\n"[39m +
                  [32m"    lines_second = res_second.text.strip().split('\\n')\n"[39m +
                  [32m'    \n'[39m +
                  [32m'    for i, line in enumerate(lines_second):\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        res = json.loads(line)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        idx, doc = repaired_docs[i]\n'[39m +
                  [32m"        if res.get('success'):\n"[39m +
                  [32m'            recovered_docs.append(doc)\n'[39m +
                  [32m'        else:\n'[39m +
                  [32m'            failed_docs.append((idx, doc))\n'[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m"print('Recovered count:', len(recovered_docs))\n"[39m +
                  [32m"print('Failed count:', len(failed_docs))\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m"recovered_ids = sorted([doc['id'] for doc in recovered_docs])\n"[39m +
                  [32m"failed_ids = sorted([doc['id'] for idx, doc in failed_docs])\n"[39m +
                  [32m'\n'[39m +
                  [32m"print('Recovered IDs:', recovered_ids)\n"[39m +
                  [32m"print('Failed IDs:', failed_ids)\n"[39m +
                  [32m'"'[39m

          name: [32m'applyDiff'[39m,
          description: [32m"This is a tool for editing files. For moving or renaming files, you should generally use the executeCommand tool with the 'mv' command instead. For larger edits, use the writeFile tool to overwrite files.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m"Before using this tool, use the readFile tool to understand the file's contents and context\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'To make a file edit, provide the following:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. path: The path to the file to modify (relative to the current working directory, or an absolute path)\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. searchContent: The text to replace (must match the file contents exactly, including all whitespace and indentation)\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. replaceContent: The edited text to replace the searchContent (must be different from searchContent)\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'By default, the tool will replace ONE occurrence of searchContent with replaceContent in the specified file. If you want to replace multiple occurrences, provide the expectedReplacements parameter with the exact number of occurrences you expect.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS FOR USING THIS TOOL:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. UNIQUENESS (when expectedReplacements is not specified): The searchContent MUST uniquely identify the specific instance you want to change. This means:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context BEFORE the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include AT LEAST 3-5 lines of context AFTER the change point\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Include all whitespace, indentation, and surrounding code exactly as it appears in the file\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. EXPECTED MATCHES: If you want to replace multiple instances:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Use the expectedReplacements parameter with the exact number of occurrences you expect to replace\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - This will replace ALL occurrences of the searchContent with the replaceContent\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - If the actual number of matches doesn't equal expectedReplacements, the edit will fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'   - This is a safety feature to prevent unintended replacements\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. VERIFICATION: Before using this tool:\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When making edits:\n'[39m +

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'executeCommand'[39m,
          description: [32m'Executes a given bash command in a persistent shell session with optional timeout, ensuring proper handling and security measures.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'IMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Before executing the command, please follow these steps:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Directory Verification:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use listFiles to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - For example, before running "mkdir foo/bar", first use listFiles to check that "foo" exists and is the intended parent directory\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Command Execution:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., cd "path with spaces/file.txt")\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Examples of proper quoting:\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd "/Users/name/My Documents" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - cd /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python "/path/with spaces/script.py" (correct)\n'[39m +
            [32m'     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Capture the output of the command.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Usage notes:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The command argument is required.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- You can specify an optional timeout in seconds (up to 300s / 5 minutes). If not specified, commands will timeout after 60s (1 minute).\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If the output exceeds 30000 characters, output will be truncated before being returned to you.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When issuing multiple commands:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple executeCommand tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run "git status" and "git diff", send a single message with two executeCommand tool calls in parallel.\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single executeCommand call with \'&&\' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m "message" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before executeCommand for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.\n'[39m +
            [32m"  - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail\n"[39m +
            [32m'  - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)\n'[39m +
            [32m"- You shall avoid use the markdown code block syntax (backtick, '`') in your command, as it will be interpreted as a command substitution.\n"[39m +
            [32m"- Before using this tool, you must first think about the context provided in <system-reminder> to understand the user's environment and tailor your commands to ensure they are compatible with their system. You must also consider if the command you need to run should be executed in a specific directory rather than the current working directory, and properly set the `cwd` if necessary.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Committing changes with git\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Only create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Git Safety Protocol:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER update the git config\n'[39m +
            [32m"- NEVER run destructive git commands (push --force, reset --hard, checkout ., restore ., clean -f, branch -D) unless the user explicitly requests these actions. Taking unauthorized destructive actions is unhelpful and can result in lost work, so it's best to ONLY run these commands when given direct instructions \n"[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it\n'[39m +

When a pre-commit hook fails, the commit did NOT happen  — so --amend would modify the PREVIOUS commit, which may result in destroying work or losing previous changes. Instead, after hook failure, fix the issue, re-stage, and create a NEW commit\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When staging files, prefer adding specific files by name rather than using "git add -A" or "git add .", which can accidentally include sensitive files (.env, credentials) or large binaries\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the executeCommand tool:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files. IMPORTANT: Never use the -uall flag as it can cause memory issues on large repos.\n'[39m +

Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. "add" means a wholly new feature, "update" means an enhancement to an existing feature, "fix" means a bug fix, etc.).\n'[39m +
            [32m'  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files\n'[39m +

            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'   Note: Replace <Share URL> with the Share URL from system information if available. If not available, omit the " | [Task](...)" part.\n'[39m +

            [32m'4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook changes, retry the commit ONCE to include these automated changes. If it fails again, it usually means a pre-commit hook is preventing the commit.             [32m'\n'[39m +

            [32m'- IMPORTANT: Do not use --no-edit with git rebase commands, as the --no-edit flag is not a valid option for git rebase.\n'[39m +
            [32m'- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit\n'[39m +

            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'# Creating pull requests\n'[39m +
            [32m'Use the gh command via the executeCommand tool for ALL GitHub-related tasks including working with issues, pull requests, checks, and releases. If given a Github URL use the gh command to get the information needed.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +

            [32m'1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel using the executeCommand tool, in order to understand the current state of the branch since it diverged from the main branch:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git status command to see all untracked files (never use -uall flag)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Check if the current branch tracks a remote branch and is up to date with the remote, so you know if you need to push to the remote\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Run a git log command and `git diff [base-branch]...HEAD` to understand the full commit history for the current branch (from the time it diverged from the base branch)\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Analyze all changes that will be included in the pull request, making sure to look at all relevant commits (NOT just the latest commit, but ALL commits that will be included in the pull request!!!), and draft a pull request title and summary:\n'[39m +

            [32m'3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands in parallel:\n'[39m +

            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m`gh pr create --title "the pr title" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'\n`[39m +

          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'useSkill'[39m,
          description: [32m'Execute a skill within the main conversation\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask about a SPECIFIC skill (e.g., "How does the pdf skill work?", "What does the commit skill do?"), you must call this tool to get detailed information about that skill.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When users ask general questions like "What skills are available?" or "List all skills", simply refer to the "Available skills" section below - do NOT call this tool.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m"This tool returns the skill's detailed instructions which you must then follow to complete the task. The instructions contain step-by-step guidance on how to perform the specific task or workflow.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'How to invoke:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Use this tool with the skill name\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Example: `skill: "pdf-processing"` - invoke the pdf-processing skill\n'[39m +
            [32m"- The tool will return the skill's instructions\n"[39m +
            [32m'- Follow the returned instructions carefully to complete the task\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Important:\n'[39m +
            [32m'- When a skill is relevant for a task OR when users ask about a SPECIFIC skill, you must invoke this tool IMMEDIATELY as your first action\n'[39m +
            [32m'- For general questions about available skills, simply refer to the "Available skills" list below without calling this tool\n'[39m +
            [32m'- NEVER just announce or mention a skill in your text response without actually calling this tool (except for general skill listing requests)\n'[39m +
            [32m'- This is a BLOCKING REQUIREMENT: invoke the relevant Skill tool BEFORE generating any other response about a specific skill or task\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Only use skills listed in "Available skills" below\n'[39m +
            [32m'- Check compatibility requirements before using a skill - ensure the skill is compatible with the current OS/environment\n'[39m +
            [32m'- After calling this tool, follow the returned instructions step by step\n'[39m +
            [32m'- The skill file location is shown in the [Location: filepath] section of each skill listing below - use this information to understand where the skill is defined\n'[39m +
            [32m"- Use the directory containing the skill's source file as the base directory for resolving any resource files mentioned in the instructions\n"[39m +
            [32m'- Proactively explore the skill directory for optional resources that enhance task completion:\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * scripts/ - executable code that agents can run (Python, Bash, JavaScript, etc.)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * references/ - on-demand documentation (REFERENCE.md, FORMS.md, domain-specific files)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  * assets/ - static resources (templates, images, data files, schemas)\n'[39m +
            [32m'  Use these resources when they exist and are relevant to the current task\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Available skills:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'- **create-skill**: Helps users create new custom agent skills. Use when the user wants to create a new skill, automate a workflow, or package specialized knowledge for reuse. [Location: /tmp/pochi-builtin-2e56a4fb1f88f581/skills/create-skill.md]\n'[39m +
            [32m'- **find-skills**: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. [Location: /tmp/pochi-builtin-2e56a4fb1f88f581/skills/find-skills.md]\n'[39m +
            [32m'- **widget-guidelines**: Use before renderWidget when the user needs a visual widget, SVG diagram, UI mockup, chart, local interactive explainer, or generative art. [Location: /tmp/pochi-builtin-2e56a4fb1f88f581/skills/widget-guidelines/SKILL.md]'[39m,

          description: [32m'- Fast content search tool that works with any codebase size\n'[39m +

            [32m'- Supports Rust regex syntax (eg. "log.*Error", "functions+w+", etc.)\n'[39m +

        {
          type: [32m'function'[39m,
          name: [32m'todoWrite'[39m,
          description: [32m'Use this tool to create and manage a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.\n'[39m +
            [32m'It also helps the user understand the progress of the task and overall progress of their requests.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## When to Use This Tool\n'[39m +
            [32m'Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Complex multi-step tasks - When a task requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Non-trivial and complex tasks - Tasks that require careful planning or multiple operations\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. User explicitly requests todo list - When the user directly asks you to use the todo list\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)\n'[39m +
            [32m'5. After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as todos. Feel free to edit the todo list based on new information.\n'[39m +
            [32m'6. After completing a task - Mark it complete and add any new follow-up tasks\n'[39m +
            [32m'7. When you start working on a new task, mark the todo as in-progress. Ideally you should only have one todo as in-progress at a time. Complete existing tasks before starting new ones.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## When NOT to Use This Tool\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'Skip using this tool when:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. There is only a single, straightforward task\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. The task is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. The task can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. The task is purely conversational or informational\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'NOTE that you should use should not use this tool if there is only one trivial task to do. In this case you are better off just doing the task directly.\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## Examples of When to Use the Todo List\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m"User: I want to add a dark mode toggle to the application settings. Make sure you run the tests and build when you're done!\n"[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: I'll help add a dark mode toggle to your application settings. Let me create a todo list to track this implementation.\n"[39m +
            [32m'*Creates todo list with the following items:*\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Create dark mode toggle component in Settings page\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Add dark mode state management (context/store)\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. Implement CSS-in-JS styles for dark theme\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. Update existing components to support theme switching\n'[39m +
            [32m'5. Run tests and build process, addressing any failures or errors that occur\n'[39m +
            [32m'*Begins working on the first task*\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'The assistant used the todo list because:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. Adding dark mode is a multi-step feature requiring UI, state management, and styling changes\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. The user explicitly requested tests and build be run afterward\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. The assistant inferred that tests and build need to pass by adding "Ensure tests and build succeed" as the final task\n'[39m +
            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'User: Help me rename the function getCwd to getCurrentWorkingDirectory across my project\n'[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: Let me first search through your codebase to find all occurrences of 'getCwd'.\n"[39m +
            [32m'*Uses grep or search tools to locate all instances of getCwd in the codebase*\n'[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: I've found 15 instances of 'getCwd' across 8 different files. Let me create a todo list to track these changes.\n"[39m +
            [32m'*Creates todo list with specific items for each file that needs updating*\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'The assistant used the todo list because:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. First, the assistant searched to understand the scope of the task\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. Upon finding multiple occurrences across different files, it determined this was a complex task with multiple steps\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. The todo list helps ensure every instance is tracked and updated systematically\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. This approach prevents missing any occurrences and maintains code consistency\n'[39m +
            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'User: I need to implement these features for my e-commerce site: user registration, product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout flow.\n'[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: I'll help implement these features. First, let's add all the features to the todo list.\n"[39m +
            [32m'*Creates a todo list breaking down each feature into specific tasks based on the project architecture*\n'[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: Let's start with user registration. This will involve creating database models, API endpoints, and frontend forms that integrate with your existing authentication system.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'The assistant used the todo list because:\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. The user provided multiple complex features to implement in a comma separated list\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. The todo list helps organize these large features into manageable tasks\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. This approach allows for tracking progress across the entire implementation\n'[39m +
            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +
            [32m"User: Can you help optimize my React application? It's rendering slowly and has performance issues.</user>\n"[39m +
            [32m"Assistant: I'll help optimize your React application. First, let me examine your codebase to identify potential performance bottlenecks.\n"[39m +

            [32m'*Creates todo list with items like: 1) Implement memoization for expensive calculations in ProductList, 2) Add virtualization for long lists in Dashboard, 3) Optimize image loading in Gallery component, 4) Fix state update loops in ShoppingCart, 5) Review bundle size and implement code splitting*\n'[39m +
            [32m"Let's start by implementing memoization for the expensive calculations in your ProductList component.</assistant>\n"[39m +

            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'The assistant used the todo list because:\n'[39m +

            [32m'2. Based on this analysis, it identified multiple optimization opportunities\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. Performance optimization is a non-trivial task requiring multiple steps\n'[39m +
            [32m'4. The todo list helps methodically track improvements across different components\n'[39m +
            [32m'5. This systematic approach ensures all performance bottlenecks are addressed\n'[39m +
            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## Examples of When NOT to Use the Todo List\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +

            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m"The assistant did not use the todo list because this is a single, trivial task that can be completed in one step. There's no need to track multiple tasks or steps for such a straightforward request.\n"[39m +
            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +

            [32m"Assistant: The git status command shows the current state of your working directory and staging area. It displays which changes have been staged, which haven't, and which files aren't being tracked by Git. This is a fundamental command for understanding the current state of your repository before making commits.\n"[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +

            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +

            [32m'<reasoning>\n'[39m +

            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +

            [32m'<example>\n'[39m +

            [32m'</reasoning>\n'[39m +
            [32m'</example>\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'## Task States and Management\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'1. **Task States**: Use these states to track progress:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - pending: Task not yet started\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - in-progress: Currently working on (limit to ONE task at a time)\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - completed: Task finished successfully\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - cancelled: Task no longer needed\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'2. **Task Management**:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Update task status in real-time as you work\n'[39m +
            [32m"   - Mark tasks complete IMMEDIATELY after finishing (don't batch completions)\n"[39m +
            [32m'   - Only have ONE task in-progress at any time\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Complete current tasks before starting new ones\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Cancel tasks that become irrelevant\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'3. **Task Breakdown**:\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Create specific, actionable items\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Break complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps\n'[39m +
            [32m'   - Use clear, descriptive task names\n'[39m +
            [32m'\n'[39m +
            [32m'When in doubt, use this tool. Being proactive with task management demonstrates attentiveness and ensures you complete all requirements successfully.'[39m,
